instruction: Prep Chicken breast: remove loose membrane and visible fat as desired. Do not rinse raw chicken. Pat dry, even the thickness, and season both sides.
Heat a lightly oiled skillet over medium to medium-high heat. Brown the first side, turn, and cook until the thickest part reaches 165°F.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and let it rest before slicing.
Slice the rested chicken breast just before serving.
Prep Chicken breast: remove packaging and season the frozen chicken breast. Use a covered or moist method unless thawed first.
Begin cooking the frozen chicken breast with a covered or moist method.
Let the frozen chicken continue cooking through without constant attention.
Verify that the thickest part of the chicken breast has reached 165°F.
Rest the chicken breast before slicing.
Slice the rested chicken breast just before serving.
Prep Chicken breast: slice, shred, or dice the cooked chicken breast as needed.
Reheat the cooked chicken breast gently in a skillet until hot; avoid drying it out.
Prep Rice: measure the rice and cooking liquid.
Put the rice and liquid in the saucepan, bring them to a simmer, then cover and reduce the heat.
Let the rice simmer covered without constant attention.
Remove the rice from heat and let it rest covered before fluffing.
Prep Mushrooms: quickly rinse attached dirt or wipe with a damp towel; do not soak. Trim dry stem ends and slice about 1/4 inch thick.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat. Cook the mushrooms in a single, uncrowded layer until their moisture evaporates and the bottoms brown. Turn and finish cooking.
Prep Asparagus: wash the asparagus and remove the woody ends. Leave the spears whole or cut them into bite-size pieces.
Heat oil or butter in a skillet. Sauté the asparagus 2–3 minutes. Add a little water and cover briefly for thick stalks. Finish until bright green and crisp-tender.
Prep Swiss chard: rinse the leaves and stems thoroughly. Separate and slice the edible stems. Leave the leaves large or tear them into manageable pieces.
Heat oil in a skillet. Cook the sliced stems first until they begin to soften. Add the leaves with a splash of liquid and toss until wilted and tender.
Serve Swiss chard promptly because it does not hold well after cooking.
